title: Armed robbers board vessel off Nigeria coast in Gulf of Guinea
summary: Armed individuals board product tanker 85nm southwest of Bayelsa State. Master reports crew musters safely, vessel proceeds. MDAT-GoG urges reporting suspicious activities.
contents:
## Armed robbers board vessel off Nigeria coast in Gulf of Guinea

### Overview
Armed individuals board product tanker 85nm southwest of Bayelsa State. Master reports crew musters safely, vessel proceeds. MDAT-GoG urges reporting suspicious activities.

### Event Details
MDAT-GoG reports armed robbers board product tanker approximately 85 nautical miles southwest of Bayelsa State, Nigeria, in Gulf of Guinea. Master activates SSAS alarm, musters crew to safe muster, repels attackers. Vessel continues passage unharmed after robbers depart empty-handed. Incident highlights persistent maritime threats in region despite international patrols. MDAT-GoG coordinates with Nigerian Navy, flags state authorities for rapid response. Crew remains safe, vessel operates normally post-incident. Authorities investigate attack patterns, urge vessels to maintain heightened vigilance, report positions promptly. Recent spike in boardings prompts enhanced advisory measures. MDAT-GoG monitors situation closely, updates followers on developments. Pirates exploit night hours, target laden tankers frequently. Vessel masters implement best management practices, including early detection via radar, AIS monitoring. Nigerian authorities strengthen offshore patrols, collaborate regionally. Incident underscores Gulf of Guinea status as global piracy hotspot. MDAT-GoG encourages masters to join UKMTO group, share intelligence real-time. Attack demonstrates ongoing risks to commercial shipping, demands collective vigilance from industry stakeholders.

### Source Context
@MDAT_GoG (MDAT Gulf of Guinea) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on gulf of guinea piracy attack nigeria armed robbery vessel boarding incident.
